name: Hugging Face Accelerate On Slurm
summary: Plan and smoke test Hugging Face Accelerate launches on Slurm.
description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm pattern for reviewing Hugging Face Accelerate multi-GPU and multi-node launch settings, including main process address, machine rank, process count, mixed precision, accelerator state, and a tiny tensor collective before running real training.

inputs.name: gpu_partition
inputs.description: Slurm partition or queue that provides GPU nodes.

inputs.name: accelerate_environment
inputs.description: Module, virtual environment, Conda environment, or container that provides accelerate and PyTorch.

inputs.name: processes_per_node
inputs.description: Number of Accelerate worker processes to launch per Slurm node, normally matching the GPU count per node for data-parallel smoke tests.

inputs.name: mixed_precision
inputs.description: Accelerate mixed precision setting such as no, fp16, or bf16.

inputs.name: main_process_port
inputs.description: TCP port used for the main Accelerate process.

outputs.name: accelerate_launch_log
outputs.description: Slurm log containing scheduler layout, selected Accelerate launch arguments, package versions, accelerator state, and smoke-test status.

outputs.name: launch_record
outputs.description: Reviewable launch plan with main process address, machine count, total process count, mixed precision, and dry-run or execution mode.

examples.title: Plan or run an Accelerate Slurm smoke test

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check the Slurm wrapper shell syntax.
Check the Python smoke script syntax without requiring Accelerate to be installed.

Replace account, partition, and environment placeholders, submit with RUN_ACCELERATE_SMOKE=0 to review the launch plan, then submit with RUN_ACCELERATE_SMOKE=1 on a short GPU allocation and confirm every process reports status=ok.
